compiled_state.title: [dso:sprint Skill tool]: Skill returns only 'Launching skill: dso:sprint' with no workflow content -> orchestrator falls back to manual operation
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview

On a host project session, invoking /dso:sprint via the Skill tool returned only the terse string 'Launching skill: dso:sprint' with no follow-on content, instructions, or epic selection prompt. The skill did not expand into its full workflow.

## Actual Behavior

Skill tool returned: 'Launching skill: dso:sprint'
No epic list, no Phase 1 initialization, no workflow instructions appeared.

The orchestrator fell back to manually running .claude/scripts/dso ticket list, filtering for open epics, finding epic 9780-448d and its 5 child tasks, verifying completion via git log, running unit tests, and closing completed tasks.

## Expected Behavior

After Skill tool invocation, the sprint skill should inject its content into context, display available open epics, and prompt the user to select one before orchestrating implementation.

## Possible Root Causes (from reporter)

1. Skill content loading failure — skill .md content failed to load (network timeout, file read error, content truncation)
2. Missing second invocation step — 'Launching skill' may be intermediate state requiring a follow-up SendMessage call
3. Empty or malformed skill file — skill definition may return stub response
4. Session startup collision — using-lockpick hook injected <command-name>/dso:sprint</command-name> block before skill loaded, causing a conflict

## Context

- Session start hook (using-lockpick) was active
- <command-message>dso:sprint</command-message> was pre-injected in the user message
- Skill tool call was the very first action taken
- This matches existing P0 bug 354b-7857 (chronic intermittent Skill content injection failure) but adds the specific 'Launching skill:' intermediate state observation as a new data point

comments.body: Covered by fix for 354b-7857: using-lockpick now explicitly handles terse 'Launching skill:' response as silent injection failure, with immediate Read tool fallback.
comments.author: Test
